items.front: What is the general rule for ATC speed adjustments?
items.back: Use the minimum speed control needed to achieve spacing, avoid unnecessary increases and decreases, and terminate speed control when it is no longer needed.

items.front: What kinds of speed instructions may ATC issue?
items.back: ATC may assign present or specific speed, a speed at or above or below a value, maximum forward speed, slowest practical speed, or an increase or reduction to a stated speed or Mach number.

items.front: What arrival speed may be assigned below Class B airspace or in a VFR corridor through Class B?
When should ATC tell an aircraft to resume normal speed?
items.back: Use resume normal speed only when ATC-assigned speed control is no longer needed and no published speed restriction still applies.
